summary.intent: Investigate and fix GitHub issue #628 where the agent auto-continue mechanism was dropping long assistant responses, replacing them with short closing summaries that got persisted to the session.
summary.outcome: Fixed both streaming and non-streaming auto-continue paths in runner.rs by gating auto-continue on a 40-char substantive text threshold, rewording the nudge prompt, and adding 5 new regression tests. All 294 tests pass; PR created after commit and push.

repo: Auto-continue logic lives in two separate paths in crates/agents/src/runner.rs: run_agent_loop (non-streaming, ~line 1135-1188) and run_agent_loop_streaming (~line 1838-1893).
Session persistence uses result.text from the runner, set via display_text in crates/chat/src/lib.rs:6879 — both the final WebSocket broadcast and session JSON come from this single field.
The existing test test_auto_continue_triggers_after_tool_calls validated the cap/iteration logic but never asserted on result.text, so the bug was untested.
just lint is blocked on macOS by a pre-existing moltis-csq llama-cpp-sys-2 CUDA build failure; per-crate clippy is the workaround.
The project uses a beads (bd) internal issue tracker alongside GitHub issues; bugs get filed in bd and linked to GH numbers.

code.finding: Non-streaming auto-continue branch: was firing even when response.text was substantive; now gated on is_substantive_answer_text().
Streaming auto-continue branch: same bug — was firing on any iteration with no tool calls regardless of accumulated_text length.
Existing last_answer_text fallback in non-streaming path was already present but only reached on the tool-call exit path, not the auto-continue path.
test_auto_continue_triggers_after_tool_calls: only asserted on iterations/tool_calls_made, never on result.text — silent regression hole.
display_text = result.text is the single source for both WebSocket final broadcast and session persistence — fixing result.text is sufficient, no UI changes needed.

workflow: The 'longest wins' approach to preserving text across auto-continue iterations broke existing tests; narrower scoping (only preserve text abandoned by an explicit auto-continue decision via a dedicated variable) was the correct fix.

Nudge prompt wording directly influenced model behavior — the old prompt said 'provide a brief final answer' which actively caused the regression; prompt engineering is a correctness concern, not just UX.
Running cargo clippy per-crate (-p moltis-agents, -p moltis-chat) is a reliable substitute when just lint is blocked by unrelated build failures.

friction: just lint is permanently broken on macOS due to a pre-existing llama-cpp-sys-2 CUDA build failure in moltis-csq, requiring per-crate clippy as a workaround.
The 'longest wins' preserve strategy had to be abandoned after it broke existing tests — required a second design iteration to scope preservation to only auto-continue-abandoned text.
bd CLI search commands (bd search '628', bd search 'auto-continue') returned no useful results, requiring manual code archaeology to find the affected lines.
open_items: The 'approval timed out' tool-error flow visible in the user's original transcript is a separate bug — explicitly deferred and not addressed in this fix.

The UI delta→final replacement behavior (streaming text deltas then a 'final' frame overwrites) is correct once result.text is fixed, but the interaction between streaming UI state and session persistence could be worth auditing independently.

The existing test test_auto_continue_triggers_after_tool_calls was updated to keep text below the 40-char threshold so it still exercises cap logic — but it could be strengthened to also assert result.text correctness.
PR not yet reviewed or merged at time of transcript end.
